body_txt: A bald character is not happy about being bald. They used to have hair, only to lose it. Often they cover it with a hat or a wig . While they may be Prematurely Bald , they might simply be getting older. The tone of this trope is largely gender-dependent. Since roughly half of all men will lose their hair to a noticeable degree by age 50, complaining about this inevitability is often considered the height of vanity, and thus this trope is often Played for Laughs with men (though not always). With women, drastic hair loss is much more likely to be Played for Drama since women are expected to have long, luscious locks , and are less likely to lose their hair barring a medical condition . Some embrace the new look , however. Sometimes caused by a Traumatic Haircut . May lead to Hair Today, Gone Tomorrow . Not to be confused with Baldness Mockery ; that's when someone else is doing the insulting. Subtrope of Appearance Angst . Contrast Bald Head of Toughness . Sister trope to Fake-Hair Drama and First Gray Hair .
